overall.description: A maritime navigation simulation showing the view from a ship's bridge at night, featuring operational radar and electronic chart displays. The vessel cruises through dark coastal waters until a lightning strike briefly illuminates the ocean horizon.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge navigation station with electronic displays

events.description: lightning strike flashes on the left horizon, briefly illuminating the ocean surface

agents.ego_description: operating the ship's navigation systems while cruising through coastal waters at night, monitoring radar and chart displays

entered.detail: electronic chart plotter showing coastal map with vessel position and depth contours
circular radar screen with rotating sweep arm displaying surrounding contacts
sonar depth finder showing water column profile and bottom structure
bow of the vessel with metal railings and deck equipment visible through bridge windows
dark water surface extending to the horizon with gentle wave movement

segments.scene_description: The view from a ship's bridge at night, with three navigation monitors displaying charts, radar, and depth data. The vessel moves steadily through dark coastal waters. At approximately 11 seconds, a lightning strike flashes on the left horizon, briefly illuminating the ocean surface before returning to darkness.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot captures a steady progression through a dark maritime environment that is momentarily transformed by a sudden burst of light. It begins from the perspective of a ship's bridge, where three navigation monitors glow with charts, radar, and depth data against the surrounding night. As the vessel cruises through the deep shadows of coastal waters, the oppressive darkness remains constant until a sharp lightning strike flashes across the left horizon. This sudden discharge briefly illuminates the vast ocean surface in a stark white glare before the scene shifts back into the heavy gloom of the night.
